sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking (16, 52) was no_impact. Let me rethink the pattern.
Looking at the history:
- I clicked the purple ring center to open paths
- I clicked the orange object M center multiple times to move it
- I clicked the purple square M center to move it
- I clicked white pixels to complete gray diamonds
But now I'm stuck. Let me look at what's remaining:
- Gray diamond id=5 at (10-14, 49-53) - 12 pixels
- Gray diamond id=16 at (18-22, 45-49) - 12 pixels
- White pixels at (15-18, 51-53) - 5 pixels
- Gray pixels at (35-37, 44-47) - 6 pixels
The pattern seems to be:
1. Click M center to move object
2. Object covers gray diamond, converting it to white
3. Click M center again to move object away, leaving white pixels
4. White pixels remain as completed marker
But I'm not sure if that's the right pattern. Let me look at the orange object at (16, 49). It's still there with M center. Maybe I need to click it again to move it somewhere else.
Actually, looking at the changes from clicking (16, 49):
- Gray pixels at (15-18, 51-53) turned white
- White pixels at (35-37, 44-47) turned gray
This suggests the orange object "transferred" the white state from one location to another. Maybe I need to click the orange object M center again to transfer it to another location.
Let me try clicking (16, 49) again to see if it moves the white pixels to a different location.
